commands.review_summary:
Adds opt-in `skillsMerge: { add, remove }` composition for agent entries and Telegram topic configs, with schema/type/generated metadata updates and focused tests.

PR surface: Source +137, Tests +241, Generated 0. Total +378 across 13 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: PR head adds `skillsMerge` to schema/types, while schema help, labels, and public docs still omit it or describe replacement-only skill filters. Real behavior proof is not present in the PR body or comments.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed because this PR adds public skill-filter config semantics, still needs contributor real behavior proof, and requires maintainer product/API acceptance.

target.title: feat: add skill allowlist merge config
target.body:
Related #97567

## What Problem This Solves

Resolves a problem where users who configure `agents.defaults.skills` or Telegram topic `skills` filters must restate the entire inherited skill allowlist whenever they only want to add or remove a small number of scoped skills.

## Why This Change Was Made

Adds an opt-in `skillsMerge: { add, remove }` config shape for agent entries and Telegram topic configs while preserving existing `skills` replacement semantics. This PR treats `skillsMerge` as a concrete proposed contract for the maintainer/product decision called out on #97567: it composes against a finite inherited filter when one exists, and otherwise starts from an empty allowlist so `remove` never means unsafe "all skills minus X".

Telegram group/direct configs keep explicit `skills` replacement only; `skillsMerge` is intentionally limited to topics there, where a real inherited group/direct or `topics["*"]` filter exists.

## User Impact

Operators can keep a shared baseline in `agents.defaults.skills`, then let an agent add project-specific skills or remove noisy baseline skills without copying the whole list. Telegram forum topics and DM topics can similarly adjust inherited skill filters without replacing the complete parent/default-topic list.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-scope.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts src/config/schema.test.ts` (3 shards passed: 58 config tests, 47 agent tests, 115 Telegram tests)
- `node node_modules/oxfmt/bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/config/types.telegram.ts src/config/zod-schema.providers-core.ts src/config/schema.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/group-config-helpers.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts src/config/bundled-channel-config-metadata.generated.ts`
- `pnpm config:channels:check`
- `node scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm tsgo:core && pnpm tsgo:extensions` (passed; local shell emitted the existing Node engine warning for v22.18.0 vs repo >=22.19.0)
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main --codex-bin .artifacts/codex-flex` (clean: no accepted/actionable findings)
target.author: aditya-vithaldas
